lesson.menu_desc: Future arrangements: I'm meeting the team on Monday + suggestions Why don't we...? / Shall we...? -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- Making Plans & Arrangements
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Aline Sberci | Aula 16 -- Making Plans & Arrangements

listenings.text: Hi Aline, this is Tom. I am calling about next week. We need to set up a planning meeting for the new project. I am flying to the Basel office on Wednesday, so Wednesday does not work for me. On Monday I am meeting the marketing team in the morning, but I am free in the afternoon. On Tuesday I have a doctor's appointment at noon. So my best slot is Monday afternoon, around three o'clock. Why don't we get together then? If that is not good for you, we can reschedule. Please confirm when you can. Thanks, and talk soon.

This is a quick voice note to plan my week before I reply to Tom. Let me check my calendar. On Monday morning I am presenting the budget, so I am busy until lunch. In the afternoon I am free, so Monday at three works well for me. On Tuesday I am working from home, and on Thursday I am traveling to the client. I will confirm Monday afternoon with Tom and set up the meeting room. If something comes up, I can postpone it to Friday. My goal is to arrange one clear time that works for both of us, and to send a calendar invite today.

extra_audio.text: First, Tom calls to set up a planning meeting for the new project. Then he explains that he is flying to Basel on Wednesday, so that day does not work. After that, he suggests Monday afternoon at three o'clock. Next, Aline checks her calendar and confirms that Monday works for her. Finally, she sends a calendar invite and books the meeting room.
